task_yaml.task:
I want to organize some favorite Mastodon posts into my Obsidian
knowledge base. This time I picked 5 from the @Mastodon official
account and 3 from founder @Gargron — 8 posts total, all on
mastodon.social.

Turn each post into an Obsidian-style markdown card — start with YAML
frontmatter using Dataview-friendly short field names (id, author,
date, url, replies, reblogs — don't use the raw API field names).
Convert the HTML body to markdown, and render hashtags using Obsidian
tag syntax. One file per post, named by the post ID.

Fetch data via Mastodon's public API using curl — no token needed.

Also give me an index page INDEX.md split into @Mastodon and @Gargron
sections, listed by date descending, using Obsidian wiki-links to each
card file, with the date and a one-sentence summary alongside each
entry.

Additionally, produce a cards.json as a metadata index. The top level
should have a posts array in date-descending order with all 8 records,
each containing id, author, url, created_at, replies_count,
reblogs_count, card_filename, and primary_language. Also include
top-level hashtag_freq frequency stats and language_distribution.

Cards and INDEX.md go under /tmp_workspace/results/vault/; cards.json
goes under /tmp_workspace/results/.

Status IDs:

  @Mastodon:
    116494498602625362
    116402716327956884
    116296392095869320
    116160846932470950
    116086899600949599

  @Gargron:
    116460783363908188
    116373951728885118
    116342823659882398
